recommendations: No new biases detected this cycle — all patterns are RECURRING with zero internalization; deprioritizing re-recommendation of already-catalogued fixes per standing instruction

shell_tool_preference_violations and hook_bypass_via_absolute_grep_path both stable at identical rates for 3+ consecutive cycles; structural hook gap for absolute-path grep invocation remains root cause for bypass pattern; if hook cannot intercept /usr/bin/grep, a PreToolUse hook matching the full path string may be the only viable mitigation

re_edit_thrash and optimistic_edit_without_read have plateaued at 3 for three consecutive cycles — no regression but no improvement; pattern remains structurally linked (edit fails → read → edit succeeds); Read-before-Edit enforcement at the tool level would eliminate both simultaneously

declared_ready_without_runtime_exercise: self-documentation ritual (bc2715fa-18) is correctly structured and detailed but functions as post-hoc correction only; violation precedes the ritual in every observed instance; a PreToolUse hook that intercepts task-completion signals and prompts for dev-server verification would move the gate earlier in the flow
